title: Melicope major
intro: Melicope major, also known as the large yellow peeper or New Zealand tea tree, is a plant species native to New Zealand and Australia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The health benefits of Melicope major include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been used traditionally to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, limonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Melicope major is often used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas and tinctures, to harness its benefits, and it is also used in modern medicine as a natural remedy for various health issues.
taxonomy: Melicope major is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This plant is native to New Zealand and is also known by other names such as the New Zealand orange or five-finger. The genus Melicope is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the Pacific region. Melicope major is a shrub or small tree that typically grows up to 5 meters in height and is known for its aromatic leaves.
